Semantic Query runtime path This is the complete end to end Semantic Query path for one turn. It follows the same Database Knowledge Graph runtime style, but each stage is specific to semantic retrieval, AST generation, deterministic SQL compile, execution, and response shaping. 1 User request Responsibility: Ingress payload reaches ConvEngine with user text, conversation id, and input params. Session Mutations: userText , inputParams , bootstrap context placeholders. Config/Table Dependencies: ce_conversation (R/W) , ce_audit (W) . } } HTTP payload is converted into an EngineSession with immutable conversation identity and mutable turn context. userText , optional inputParams , and request metadata are attached before any classifier or rule executes. Session bootstrap ensures downstream steps read and mutate a shared context map. Conversation state is loaded if conversation id exists; otherwise first turn bootstrap is created. Audit context is opened so stage level determinants are linked to this turn. 2 McpToolStep + McpPlanner Responsibility: Planner resolves semantic turn to CALL_TOOL with tool_code=db.semantic.query . Session Mutations: context.mcp.lifecycle. , MCP_ACTION , MCP_TOOL_CODE , planner observations. Config/Table Dependencies: ce_mcp_planner (R) , ce_mcp_tool (R) , ce_rule (R) , ce_audit (W) . McpToolStep runs PRE_AGENT_MCP rules so semantic flow can be state gated. Enabled MCP tools are fetched for current intent and state. McpPlanner receives tool list + observations + context and emits strict JSON action. On CALL_TOOL , selected tool executes and observation is appended into MCP context. Loop continues until planner returns ANSWER , then mcp.finalAnswer is persisted. 3 DbSemanticQueryToolHandler Responsibility: Bridge MCP DB tool invocation to semantic runtime. Session Mutations: Question normalized from args/session. Config/Table Dependencies: convengine.mcp.db.semantic. , ce_audit (W) . } } Handler is resolved by MCP DB executor by tool code. Question extraction order is deterministic: question query user_input session.userText . Handler intentionally avoids planner/retrieval/SQL logic; it is an adapter boundary. Delegates to SemanticQueryRuntimeService.plan with normalized question. This keeps runtime swappable without changing MCP core contracts. 4 SemanticQueryRuntimeService Responsibility: Orchestrate semantic stage pipeline and handle runtime errors. Session Mutations: retrieval , joinPath , ast , canonicalAst , compiledSql , execution , summary , _meta , _db . Config/Table Dependencies: mode and enable flags, ce_audit (W) , ce_verbose (R) . catch (Exception stageError) } } Runtime rejects call unless mcp.db.query.mode=semantic and semantic is enabled. Interceptors are sorted once and wrapped around each stage. DAG ordered stages execute deterministically. Stage failures emit stage specific error determinants with rich metadata. Final output contains stage artifacts for troubleshooting and UI trace rendering. 5 SemanticRetrievalStage Responsibility: Retrieve semantic entity and candidate tables. Session Mutations: retrieval.candidateEntities , retrieval.candidateTables , confidence. Config/Table Dependencies: semantic layer.yaml , optional embeddings/vector search, ce_audit . Retrieval is the stage that decides which semantic entity and tables the rest of the pipeline should trust. Everything downstream (join path, AST quality, SQL compile quality) depends on this ranking. Activation YAML Behind the scenes workflow (no vector) Resolve retriever bean via @Order + supports(session) . Tokenize question to normalized lowercase terms. For each semantic entity: compute deterministic score using synonyms + fields + lexical + id pattern. For selected entities, score candidate tables using table name + field column lexical overlap. Apply thresholds + top N limits and write retrieval.candidateEntities and retrieval.candidateTables . Behind the scenes workflow (vector enabled) Everything from no vector mode still runs. Generate embedding for user question using llmClient.generateEmbedding(question) . Run pgvector lookup via PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter for nearest ENTITY and TABLE rows. Blend deterministic score and vector score per candidate. If feedback vectors are enabled, apply additional USER_QUERY similarity boost ( feedbackVector ). Sort final blended scores and output final candidate lists. How lexical matching works (with your semantic YAML) matches service termination synonym."], ["Field overlap", "question tokens vs field keys + field descriptions + column names", "Question has downstream status matches downstreamStatus field."], ["Description lexical overlap", "question tokens vs entity description text", "Question has failed disconnect overlaps entity description words."], ["ID pattern boosts", "regex in question ( ZPR , DON , ZPDISC )", " DON9001 boosts disconnect/order related entity."] ]} / Score blending concept Entity/table ranking uses weighted blending from config. Turn by turn mini example Key point: Retrieval is a ranking stage. Vector does not replace lexical logic; it augments it to improve semantic recall. The output from this stage ( candidateEntities , candidateTables , reasons, confidence) is the contract consumed by join path and AST stages. 6 SemanticJoinPathStage Responsibility: Resolve join path using schema graph traversal. Session Mutations: joinPath.baseTable , joinPath.edges , joinPath.requiredTables , joinPath.unresolvedTables . Config/Table Dependencies: graph adapter ( jgrapht ), DB FK metadata, ce_audit . } } } Base table is picked from top candidate table from retrieval stage. Required tables are resolved from selected candidates. Shortest path traversal is run per target table under maxJoinHops . Resolved edges are merged without duplicates. Unresolved tables are captured and later enforced by AST validator. 7 SemanticAstGenerationStage Responsibility: Generate strict JSON AST using LLM. Session Mutations: astRawJson , astVersion , SemanticQueryAstV1 , canonicalAst . Config/Table Dependencies: ce_config prompt keys for DefaultSemanticAstGenerator , ce_audit . } Stage runs only after retrieval + join path success. Prompt context includes question, selected entity, allowed entities, candidate tables, join path, and context json. Schema JSON is entity specialized so generated fields stay within selected semantic entity. LLM output is strict JSON; SQL text output is not accepted in this stage. Raw JSON is normalized and audited via AST_INPUT , AST_OUTPUT , AST_ERROR determinants. 8 SemanticAstValidationStage Responsibility: Validate canonical AST against semantic contracts. Session Mutations: astValidation.valid , astValidation.errors . Config/Table Dependencies: semantic model fields/metrics/rules, ce_audit . } Entity must exist in semantic model; unknown entity fails immediately. Projection/group/sort/filter field keys are validated against semantic fields. Operator value shape is validated ( IN , BETWEEN , null check operators, etc.). Exists/subquery/window clauses are recursively validated. Unresolved join path tables and limit/offset constraints are enforced pre compile. 9 SemanticSqlCompileStage Responsibility: Deterministic AST parameterized SQL compile + policy validation. Session Mutations: compiledSql , compiledSqlParams , sqlCompiled=true . Config/Table Dependencies: semantic model mappings, SQL clause/operator/window handlers, McpSqlGuardrail , ce_audit . Compile stage runs only after AST validation is successful. Compiler uses canonical AST and semantic model to resolve physical columns/tables. Clause handler registries apply predicates, metrics, windows, exists, and subqueries. Output SQL is always parameterized with named bind values. Policy validator applies read only guardrail before execution stage. 10 SemanticSqlExecuteStage Responsibility: Execute compiled SQL and map result rows. Session Mutations: execution.rowCount , execution.rows[] , _db.sql , _db.params . Config/Table Dependencies: datasource/JDBC, domain tables (read only), ce_audit . Stage fails fast if SQL compile output is missing. Named parameter SQL executes through NamedParameterJdbcTemplate . Result rows are copied into immutable execution payload. Audit payload captures SQL and bind params under _db for debugging. DB grammar/type/permission errors are surfaced as SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR with root cause. 11 SemanticResultSummaryStage Responsibility: Build deterministic text/markdown summary from result rows. Session Mutations: summary . Config/Table Dependencies: execution result data, ce_audit . No result branch returns deterministic string, not LLM generated prose. Result table branch extracts headers from first row keys. Rows are serialized as markdown table to render in ConvEngine UI/docs trace. Cell escape logic keeps table valid for symbols/newlines. Summary preview + length are emitted into audit payload. 12 McpPlanner + RulesStep (POST_AGENT_MCP) Responsibility: Planner finalizes answer and rules complete post MCP transitions. Session Mutations: mcp.finalAnswer , outcome/status flags, state transitions. Config/Table Dependencies: ce_mcp_planner , ce_rule , ce_audit . } After semantic execution, planner receives fresh observations and decides final answer text. mcp.finalAnswer is stored in context and input params. POST_AGENT_MCP rule phase runs to move workflow state. Rule actions can map to success/failure/reset states. Planner and rule outcomes are auditable for RCA. 13 ResponseResolutionStep + Assistant output Responsibility: Resolve final response mapping and emit assistant payload. Session Mutations: OutputPayload + persisted turn output. Config/Table Dependencies: ce_response , ce_prompt_template , ce_output_schema (optional), ce_conversation , ce_audit . Response row is selected by intent/state and priority. Response type handler renders EXACT/DERIVED payload accordingly. Output transformers are applied if configured. Final payload is attached to session and returned as assistant turn output. Assistant output determinant closes the turn level audit trail. Semantic Model YAML ( semantic layer.yaml ) Semantic mode relies on a declarative semantic model file that maps business language to physical schema. Retrieval, AST validation, join path planning, metric compilation, and guardrails all consume this file. mcp.db.semantic.model path=classpath:/mcp/semantic layer.yaml Complete sample semantic model semantic layer.yaml This section explains every major tag in the full sample and what ConvEngine actually does with it at runtime. SemanticModelLoader is configured with FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false . That means unknown/unmapped YAML tags are ignored safely at load time. 1) Model identity tags What this does: version : surfaces as semanticModelVersion in runtime output and stage payloads. database + description : metadata/context only; not a SQL generator by itself. 2) entities block How each child tag is used: ", "retrieval + validator", "Becomes candidate entity and AST entity target."], ["description", "retrieval + LLM prompt", "Improves retrieval relevance and LLM grounding context."], ["synonyms", "retrieval (+ embedding text)", "Raises deterministic synonym score for candidate ranking."], ["tables.primary", "planner/compiler/validator", "Primary base table candidate for joins and projections."], ["tables.related", "retrieval/planner", "Expands table candidates for join path resolution."], ["fields. .column", "compiler", "Maps semantic field key to physical table.column in SQL select/where/order/group."], ["fields. .type", "validator", "Operator/type validation (e.g., ILIKE requires string like field)."], ["fields. .description", "retrieval", "Extra lexical signal for entity/field matching."], ["fields. .aliases", "normalizer + LLM prompt context", "Maps user/LLM variants (for example id/status/created) to canonical semantic field keys."], ["fields. .filterable/searchable/key", "currently metadata", "Carried in model; not strict query gating in current validator/compiler."] ]} / Example: User asks: show requests where teamNotes contains billing Retrieval picks DisconnectRequest (field ownership + lexical/synonym/vector signals). AST schema specialization restricts allowed field keys for selected entity. Compiler uses teamNotes zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes and, if filters reference another table owned field (for example requestId zp_request.zp_request_id ), compile stage auto expands required tables and adds the join path. 3) tables + columns + foreign_key What this does: Provides table/column metadata for schema validation and graph wiring. primary_key and foreign_key are model level structure hints. foreign_key.table/column can contribute to relationship sanity and graph traversal completeness. 4) relationships to: type: one_to_many } What this does: Join path stage builds traversal edges from these mappings. SQL compiler uses edges to generate deterministic joins. Correlation predicates for EXISTS/subqueries also rely on relationship knowledge. 5) metrics What this does: Metric key becomes allowed value in AST metrics[] . Metric SQL is inserted by compiler when aggregation is requested. Having clause can reference metric keys. Critical rule: If metrics is present with detail projections, selected fields must be grouped ( group_by ) or query is invalid. 6) synonyms (top level) Current behavior: Loaded into model and available for future expansion. Main retrieval scoring today relies primarily on entity level synonyms + fields + descriptions. 7) value_patterns + intent_rules (deterministic bridge for ambiguous asks) What this does at runtime: match_any + must_contain gates when rule activates. force_entity overrides LLM selected entity. force_mode: detail drops accidental aggregate pieces ( metrics , having , group_by ). force_mode_even_with_metrics: true allows this rule to force detail/select even if AST contains metrics . force_select sets deterministic projection for that business intent. enforce_where injects deterministic filters. Global value_patterns applies deterministic filter field remap based on value prefix (example requestId + DON disconnectOrderId ) without hardcoding in framework. Rule level field_remaps is still supported for rule specific overrides when needed. SemanticField supports optional aliases in YAML. SemanticModel supports top level value_patterns (global remaps), and SemanticIntentRule supports optional field_remaps (rule specific remaps), all mapped via SemanticIntentFieldRemap . 8) settings , join_hints , rules , and other descriptive tags Current status: These tags are excellent documentation for model authors. They are not currently bound to SemanticModel records, so runtime ignores them. Effective runtime limit/timezone/dialect come from application.yml ( convengine.mcp.db.semantic. ). DML/DDL guardrails are enforced by SQL policy/guardrail steps, not YAML rules . Other descriptive tags in sample: example_questions , allowed_values , example are currently prompt/documentation aids and are not strict runtime enforced model fields. AST + SQL impact walkthrough User question: show billbank gaps with request and order details LLM generated AST (problem shape): ], "groups": [] } } } Why validator rejects: Metrics + detailed select fields requires grouping. With empty group_by , SQL semantics are invalid. With current normalizer fix: Mixed aggregate/detail AST is demoted to detail query. metrics/having/group_by are removed deterministically before validation. } } Typical SQL shape after fix: If you want aggregate output intentionally, prompt explicitly: show billbank gap count by accountId Semantic Query Tool Runtime Deep Dive This section explains the runtime tool path that starts at db.semantic.query and executes retrieval join path AST SQL summary in deterministic stage order. AST Deep Dive This section explains the AST package folder by folder and how one user sentence is transformed into deterministic SQL safe structure. The AST layer is the semantic contract between LLM output and SQL compiler input. LLM can propose shape. AST layer normalizes, canonicalizes, and validates that shape. SQL compiler never compiles raw user text. Folder map and runtime responsibility Step by step with one real conversation User ask: show billbank gaps with request and order details 1) ast/core : parse raw JSON into typed AST ( SemanticQueryAstV1 ) What it means: Every top level query intent is explicit and typed. This avoids dynamic map parsing later in compiler stages. 2) ast/core : operator normalization ( AstFilter , AstSubqueryFilter ) What it means: LLM/operator variants ( contains , IS NOT , NOT IN ) are normalized before validation. Aggregate style tokens ( COUNT_GT , SUM_GTE , etc.) are normalized to base operators. Prevents enum failures from natural language operator tokens. 3) ast/normalize : deterministic cleanup + intent rule application // fail fast when normalization drops all intent critical predicates/selects assertNormalizationDidNotDropCriticalIntent(ast, normalized, targetEntity); } What it means: Maps field aliases to canonical semantic field keys. Drops unknown fields that do not belong to chosen entity (including select and projections ). Applies deterministic business overrides through intent_rules . Fails fast when normalization drops all predicates or selected fields, instead of compiling broad fallback SQL. 4) behavior change now implemented (important) if (allowDetailForce && rule != null && rule.forceSelect() != null) boolean forceDetail = allowDetailForce && rule != null && "detail".equalsIgnoreCase(rule.forceMode()); } What it means: If AST carries metrics , detail force is skipped by default. Per rule override: when force_mode_even_with_metrics: true , force_select and force_mode=detail are still allowed. For explicit metric name asks (for example show billbank_gap_count ), normalizer now preserves metric intent by clearing accidental detail projections. Projection drop guard allows metric only ASTs (non empty metrics with empty select/projections ) so aggregate queries are not falsely rejected. SQL compiler also treats metric only AST as metric only SELECT and qualifies metric SQL table names to query aliases ( zp_ t ) to avoid PostgreSQL FROM clause errors. This keeps aggregation safe globally while enabling explicit business intent exceptions. 5) ast/version : V1 to canonical mapping ( V1CanonicalAstMapper ) What it means: Compiler receives one strict shape ( CanonicalAst ) regardless of LLM formatting quirks. Mapping stage is where unsupported operators are rejected with explicit error. 6) ast/canonical : compiler safe model } What it means: This is the only AST consumed by SQL compile. Keeps compilation deterministic and isolated from prompt level noise. 7) ast/validate : strict semantic contract enforcement What it means: Unknown entity/field/operator/type mismatch fails early. Prevents invalid SQL from being generated downstream. How this AST becomes SQL (bridge summary) Real AST example and normalized effect } } What happens when AST is missing If LLM fails to produce valid AST JSON: AST_ERROR / AST_GENERATION_ERROR is emitted. Runtime returns controlled semantic failure (no SQL compile/execute). RUNTIME_ERROR captures final failure envelope. Post feedback + rebuild embeddings Use this flow to validate that thumbs feedback is now improving semantic retrieval ranking. 1. Apply required tables in DB: 2. Required application.yml : 3. Enable semantic vector mode: mcp.db.query.mode=semantic mcp.db.semantic.enabled=true mcp.db.semantic.vector.enabled=true mcp.db.semantic.model path=classpath:/mcp/semantic layer.yaml 4. Rebuild base semantic embeddings from YAML: } 5. Run a baseline semantic query: Show failed disconnect requests in last 24 hours 6. Send thumbs up feedback for that conversation: } 7. Assistant output", stage: "ASSISTANT_OUTPUT", summary: "Final response resolved and returned.", session: ["response payload"], tables: ["ce_response (R)", "ce_prompt_template (R)", "ce_conversation (W)", "ce_audit (W)"] } }; # MCP Semantic Query ( db.semantic.query ) This page documents the semantic-query engine under: com.github.salilvnair.convengine.engine.mcp.query.semantic It is the Text-to-AST-to-SQL runtime for ConvEngine MCP mode semantic . Semantic Query generates AST JSON with LLM, compiles SQL deterministically in Java, enforces policy guardrails, executes read-only SQL, and returns auditable result summaries. ## Runtime request flow ## Stage pipeline (actual order) ## Package Responsibilities canonical mapping", "SemanticQueryAstV1, AstFilterGroup, DefaultAstValidator, V1CanonicalAstMapper"], ["retrieval/*", "Entity/table retrieval models + retrievers + vector adapters", "CandidateEntity, RetrievalResult, DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever, PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter"], ["graph/*", "Schema graph + join path planning contracts/providers", "SchemaGraphEngine, JoinPathResolver, JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine, DefaultJoinPathResolver"], ["llm/*", "LLM AST JSON generation", "DefaultSemanticAstGenerator"], ["sql/*", "AST->SQL compile core + clause/operator/window handlers + policy", "DefaultSemanticSqlCompiler, DefaultAstQueryAssemblyClauseHandler, DefaultInAstOperatorHandler"], ["execute/*", "Compiled SQL execution", "DefaultSemanticSqlExecutor"], ["summary/*", "Result formatting", "DefaultSemanticResultSummarizer"], ["runtime/*", "MCP tool entry + pipeline + stage orchestration", "DbSemanticQueryToolHandler, SemanticQueryRuntimeService, SemanticStagePipelineFactory"], ["model/*", "Semantic model loading and registry", "SemanticModelLoader, SemanticModelRegistry"] ]} /> ## Libraries we use Semantic Query runtime is built with a small set of focused libraries in convengine/pom.xml . ### Core dependencies (actual) xml org.jooq jooq org.jgrapht jgrapht-core 1.5.2 com.fasterxml.jackson.dataformat jackson-dataformat-yaml ## jOOQ in Semantic Query jOOQ is not used as a codegen-first ORM here. It is used as a deterministic SQL construction layer controlled by the semantic compiler pipeline. Flow: 1. LLM generates AST JSON. 2. AST is canonicalized and validated. 3. SQL handlers build clauses with jOOQ DSL + controlled SQL tokens. 4. SQL is emitted with named params. 5. JDBC executes with bind values. ### jOOQ conversion examples json { "entity": "DisconnectRequest", "where": { "op": "AND", "conditions": [ {"field": "requestStatus", "op": "EQ", "value": "FAILED"}, {"field": "teamNotes", "op": "ILIKE", "value": "%inventory%"} ], "groups": [] } } java // DefaultComparisonAstOperatorHandler return DSL.condition("{0} " + sqlToken(operator) + " {1}", field, context.nextParam(value)); sql ... WHERE ("t1"."request_status" = :p1 AND "t2"."zp_asr_team_notes" ILIKE :p2) json { "entity": "DisconnectRequest", "where": { "op": "AND", "conditions": [ {"field": "accountId", "op": "IN", "value": ["UPSA100", "ACMA200"]}, {"field": "requestedAt", "op": "BETWEEN", "value": ["2026-03-01T00:00:00Z", "2026-03-05T00:00:00Z"]}, {"field": "disconnectId", "op": "IS_NULL", "value": null} ], "groups": [] } } java // DefaultInAstOperatorHandler + DefaultBetweenAstOperatorHandler + DefaultNullCheckAstOperatorHandler field.in(params) DSL.condition("{0} BETWEEN {1} AND {2}", field, context.nextParam(v1), context.nextParam(v2)) field.isNull() sql ... WHERE ("t1"."account_id" IN (:p1, :p2) AND "t1"."requested_at" BETWEEN :p3 AND :p4 AND "t0"."zp_disconnect_id" IS NULL) json { "entity": "DisconnectRequest", "windows": [ { "name": "account_rank", "function": "ROW_NUMBER", "partition_by": ["accountId"], "order_by": [{"field": "requestedAt", "direction": "DESC", "nulls": "LAST"}] } ] } java // DefaultRowNumberWindowFunctionHandler return "ROW_NUMBER() OVER (" + overClause + ")"; sql ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY "t1"."account_id" ORDER BY "t1"."requested_at" DESC NULLS LAST) AS "account_rank" ## JGraphT in Semantic Query JGraphT powers schema traversal and shortest join-path resolution. It prevents LLM from inventing joins by computing paths from real FK metadata. Flow: 1. Graph engine refreshes vertices/edges from schema inspector FK joins. 2. Join-path resolver chooses base table and initial required tables from retrieval. 3. SQL compile expands required tables from AST references (projections, where , having , exists , subquery , sort , group_by , time_range , metrics) before final join assembly. 4. Shortest paths are found with hop limits. 5. Resolved edges are de-duplicated and returned to compiler. ### JGraphT traversal examples java // JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.refreshGraph() for (Map row : joins) { String sourceTable = text(row.get("source_table")); String targetTable = text(row.get("target_table")); nextGraph.addVertex(sourceTable); nextGraph.addVertex(targetTable); nextGraph.addEdge(sourceTable, targetTable); nextGraph.addEdge(targetTable, sourceTable); // reverse edge } text Result: directed graph with forward + reverse FK edges for traversal flexibility. java // JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.shortestPath() while (!queue.isEmpty()) { NodePath current = queue.poll(); if (current.node().equals(toTable)) { return toEdges(current.path()); } for (String next : neighbors(current.node())) { if (!visited.contains(next)) { queue.add(new NodePath(next, nextPath)); visited.add(next); } } } text Uses BFS-style traversal; returns concrete SchemaEdge list for SQL joins. java // DefaultJoinPathResolver.doResolve() String base = candidates.get(0).name(); for (String target : requiredTables) { List path = graph.shortestPath(base, target, maxHops); if (path == null || path.isEmpty()) unresolved.add(target); else merge(path); } return new JoinPathPlan(base, merged, requiredTables, unresolved, confidence); text Compiler receives resolved edges and unresolved table list; validator blocks unresolved joins. ## AST model capabilities ### Operators in AST model AstOperator.java includes: - ILIKE - BETWEEN - IN - NOT_IN - IS_NULL - IS_NOT_NULL Normalization behavior in AstFilter / AstSubqueryFilter also accepts aggregate-style tokens and maps to base operators: - COUNT_GT -> GT - SUM_GTE -> GTE - COUNT_NOT_IN -> NOT_IN - *_NOTNULL -> IS_NOT_NULL ### New AST nodes - Boolean tree: AstFilterGroup.java - Exists: AstExistsBlock.java - Subquery filters: AstSubqueryFilter.java , AstSubquerySpec.java - Windows: AstWindowSpec.java - Full v1 payload: SemanticQueryAstV1.java ### Correlated field reference rule For exists.where and subquery_filters.where , a value starting with $ is treated as an outer-field reference, not a string literal. - $disconnectId means "compare against outer query field disconnectId " - Resolver maps that field using outer entity semantic field mapping + outer aliases Example AST: json { "exists": [ { "entity": "DisconnectRequest", "where": { "op": "AND", "conditions": [ { "field": "disconnectId", "op": "=", "value": "$disconnectId" } ], "groups": [] }, "not_exists": false } ] } Compiled SQL shape: sql EXISTS ( SELECT 1 FROM zp_request ex1 LEFT JOIN zp_disconnect_order exj2 ON ex1.zp_request_id = exj2.zp_request_id WHERE exj2.zp_disconnect_id = t0.zp_disconnect_id ) ### Validation coverage DefaultAstValidator.java validates: - entity and field existence - operator and value compatibility ( ILIKE string-only, BETWEEN two values, IN / NOT_IN array) - exists blocks - subquery filters - window specs - groupBy/metric constraints ### SQL rendering coverage - LIKE/comparison: DefaultComparisonAstOperatorHandler.java - BETWEEN: DefaultBetweenAstOperatorHandler.java - IN/NOT_IN: DefaultInAstOperatorHandler.java - IS_NULL/IS_NOT_NULL: DefaultNullCheckAstOperatorHandler.java - ROW_NUMBER window: DefaultRowNumberWindowFunctionHandler.java ### DATE_TRUNC status Not active yet: - Placeholder exists in DefaultFunctionClauseHandler.java - Missing concrete AST field + validator + function handler for DATE_TRUNC ## Static config and runtime config requirements ### Static DB rows required ### Application YAML required yaml mcp: db: query: mode: semantic # semantic-catalog | knowledge-graph | semantic semantic-catalog: enabled: true knowledge-graph: enabled: true semantic: enabled: true model-path: classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml ## Request flow by package (quick reference) 1. runtime/handler : DbSemanticQueryToolHandler receives db.semantic.query . 2. runtime/core + runtime/pipeline + runtime/stage/* : SemanticQueryRuntimeService runs stage DAG. 3. retrieval/* : ranks candidate entity/tables. 4. graph/* : resolves join edges/path. 5. llm/* : generates AST JSON. 6. ast/* : canonicalize + normalize + validate. 7. sql/* : deterministic compile + policy. 8. execute/* : SQL run + row map. 9. summary/* : final user-facing summary payload. 10. model/* : shared semantic-layer model registry. ## Semantic Query runtime path
This is the complete end-to-end Semantic Query path for one turn. It follows the same Database Knowledge Graph runtime style, but each stage is specific to semantic retrieval, AST generation, deterministic SQL compile, execution, and response shaping.
1 User request
**Responsibility:** Ingress payload reaches ConvEngine with user text, conversation id, and input params. **Session Mutations:** userText , inputParams , bootstrap context placeholders. **Config/Table Dependencies:** ce_conversation (R/W) , ce_audit (W) . { public ResponseEntity message(@RequestBody ConversationRequest request) { return ResponseEntity.ok(conversationalEngine.process(request)); } } { public ConversationResponse process(ConversationRequest request) { EngineSession session = sessionManager.loadOrCreate(request); enginePipelineFactory.getPipeline().execute(session); return session.toResponse(); } }
  1. HTTP payload is converted into an EngineSession with immutable conversation identity and mutable turn context.
  2. userText , optional inputParams , and request metadata are attached before any classifier or rule executes.
  3. Session bootstrap ensures downstream steps read and mutate a shared context map.
  4. Conversation state is loaded if conversation id exists; otherwise first-turn bootstrap is created.
  5. Audit context is opened so stage-level determinants are linked to this turn.
 ---
2 McpToolStep + McpPlanner
**Responsibility:** Planner resolves semantic turn to CALL_TOOL with tool_code=db.semantic.query . **Session Mutations:** context.mcp.lifecycle.* , MCP_ACTION , MCP_TOOL_CODE , planner observations. **Config/Table Dependencies:** ce_mcp_planner (R) , ce_mcp_tool (R) , ce_rule (R) , ce_audit (W) . { for (int i = 0; i < maxLoops; i++) { McpPlan plan = planner.plan(session, tools, observations); if (McpConstants.ACTION_ANSWER.equalsIgnoreCase(plan.action())) { writeFinalAnswerToContext(session, plan.answer()); break; } CeMcpTool tool = registry.requireTool(plan.tool_code(), session.getIntent(), session.getState()); String rowsJson = resolveExecutor(toolGroup).execute(tool, args, session); observations.add(new McpObservation(toolCode, rowsJson)); } } { PromptTemplateContext ctx = PromptTemplateContext.builder() .templateName("McpPlanner") .mcpTools(toolsJson) .mcpObservations(obsJson) .build(); String planJson = llm.generateJsonStrict(...); return normalizePlan(mapper.readValue(planJson, McpPlan.class)); }
  1. McpToolStep runs PRE_AGENT_MCP rules so semantic flow can be state-gated.
  2. Enabled MCP tools are fetched for current intent and state.
  3. McpPlanner receives tool list + observations + context and emits strict JSON action.
  4. On CALL_TOOL , selected tool executes and observation is appended into MCP context.
  5. Loop continues until planner returns ANSWER , then mcp.finalAnswer is persisted.
 ---
3 DbSemanticQueryToolHandler
**Responsibility:** Bridge MCP DB tool invocation to semantic runtime. **Session Mutations:** Question normalized from args/session. **Config/Table Dependencies:** convengine.mcp.db.semantic.* , ce_audit (W) . { public String toolCode() { ConvEngineMcpConfig.Db.Semantic cfg = ...; return cfg.getToolCode() == null || cfg.getToolCode().isBlank() ? "db.semantic.query" : cfg.getToolCode(); } } { public Object execute(CeMcpTool tool, Map args, EngineSession session) { String question = extractQuestion(args, session); return runtimeService.plan(question, session); } }
  1. Handler is resolved by MCP DB executor by tool code.
  2. Question extraction order is deterministic: question -> query -> user_input -> session.userText .
  3. Handler intentionally avoids planner/retrieval/SQL logic; it is an adapter boundary.
  4. Delegates to SemanticQueryRuntimeService.plan with normalized question.
  5. This keeps runtime swappable without changing MCP core contracts.
 ---
4SemanticQueryRuntimeService
**Responsibility:** Orchestrate semantic stage pipeline and handle runtime errors. **Session Mutations:** retrieval , joinPath , ast , canonicalAst , compiledSql , execution , summary , _meta , _db . **Config/Table Dependencies:** mode and enable flags, ce_audit (W) , ce_verbose (R) . { for (SemanticQueryStage stage : stagePipeline.stages()) { if (stage == null || !stage.supports(context)) continue; publishBefore(stageInterceptors, stage.stageCode(), session, context); try { stage.execute(context); publishAfter(stageInterceptors, stage.stageCode(), session, context); } catch (Exception stageError) { publishStageError(stage, context, session, stageError); throw stageError; } } } { List ordered = dagOrderer.order( discoveredStages, SemanticQueryStage.class, "Semantic query stage pipeline", c -> false ); this.pipeline = new SemanticStagePipeline(ordered); }
  1. Runtime rejects call unless mcp.db.query.mode=semantic and semantic is enabled.
  2. Interceptors are sorted once and wrapped around each stage.
  3. DAG-ordered stages execute deterministically.
  4. Stage failures emit stage-specific error determinants with rich metadata.
  5. Final output contains stage artifacts for troubleshooting and UI trace rendering.
 ---
5SemanticRetrievalStage
**Responsibility:** Retrieve semantic entity and candidate tables. **Session Mutations:** retrieval.candidateEntities , retrieval.candidateTables , confidence. **Config/Table Dependencies:** semantic-layer.yaml , optional embeddings/vector search, ce_audit . { SemanticEntityTableRetriever retriever = resolveRetriever(context); RetrievalResult retrieval = retriever.retrieve(context.question(), context.session()); context.retrieval(retrieval); } { double deterministic = rc.getSynonymWeight() * synonym + rc.getFieldWeight() * fields + rc.getIdPatternWeight() * idPattern + rc.getLexicalWeight() * lexical; double total = rc.getDeterministicBlendWeight() * clamp01(deterministic) + rc.getVectorBlendWeight() * clamp01(vector); }

Retrieval is the stage that decides which semantic entity and tables the rest of the pipeline should trust. Everything downstream (join-path, AST quality, SQL compile quality) depends on this ranking.

Activation YAML

{ mcp: db: query: mode: semantic semantic: enabled: true model-path: classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml vector: enabled: true feedback-enabled: true table: ce_mcp_semantic_embedding namespace-column: namespace target-type-column: target_type target-name-column: target_name embedding-column: embedding metadata-column: metadata_json max-results: 20 }

Behind-the-scenes workflow (no vector)

  1. Resolve retriever bean via @Order + supports(session) .
  2. Tokenize question to normalized lowercase terms.
  3. For each semantic entity: compute deterministic score using synonyms + fields + lexical + id-pattern.
  4. For selected entities, score candidate tables using table-name + field-column lexical overlap.
  5. Apply thresholds + top-N limits and write retrieval.candidateEntities and retrieval.candidateTables .

Behind-the-scenes workflow (vector enabled)

  1. Everything from no-vector mode still runs.
  2. Generate embedding for user question using llmClient.generateEmbedding(question) .
  3. Run pgvector lookup via PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter for nearest ENTITY and TABLE rows.
  4. Blend deterministic score and vector score per candidate.
  5. If feedback vectors are enabled, apply additional USER_QUERY similarity boost ( feedbackVector ).
  6. Sort final blended scores and output final candidate lists.

How lexical matching works (with your semantic YAML)

{ entities: DisconnectRequest: synonyms: - disconnect - disconnection - service termination fields: requestId: column: zp_request.zp_request_id description: unique request id downstreamStatus: column: zp_disconnect_order.downstream_status description: downstream orchestration status } matches service termination synonym."], ["Field overlap", "question tokens vs field keys + field descriptions + column names", "Question has downstream status -> matches downstreamStatus field."], ["Description lexical overlap", "question tokens vs entity description text", "Question has failed disconnect -> overlaps entity description words."], ["ID pattern boosts", "regex in question ( ZPR* , DON* , ZPDISC* )", " DON9001 boosts disconnect/order-related entity."] ]} />

Score blending concept

Entity/table ranking uses weighted blending from config.

{ double deterministic = synonymWeight * synonym + fieldWeight * fields + idPatternWeight * idPattern + lexicalWeight * lexical; double total = deterministicBlendWeight * clamp01(deterministic) + vectorBlendWeight * clamp01(vectorScore) + feedbackWeight * clamp01(feedbackVectorScore); }

Turn-by-turn mini example

Key point: Retrieval is a ranking stage. Vector does not replace lexical logic; it augments it to improve semantic recall. The output from this stage ( candidateEntities , candidateTables , reasons, confidence) is the contract consumed by join-path and AST stages.

---
6SemanticJoinPathStage
**Responsibility:** Resolve join path using schema graph traversal. **Session Mutations:** joinPath.baseTable , joinPath.edges , joinPath.requiredTables , joinPath.unresolvedTables . **Config/Table Dependencies:** graph adapter ( jgrapht ), DB FK metadata, ce_audit . { String base = candidates.get(0).name(); for (String target : requiredTables) { if (base.equalsIgnoreCase(target)) continue; List path = graph.shortestPath(base, target, maxHops); if (path == null || path.isEmpty()) unresolved.add(target); } } { while (!queue.isEmpty()) { NodePath current = queue.poll(); if (current.node().equals(toTable)) return toEdges(current.path()); for (String next : neighbors(current.node())) { if (!visited.contains(next)) queue.add(new NodePath(next, nextPath)); } } }
  1. Base table is picked from top candidate table from retrieval stage.
  2. Required tables are resolved from selected candidates.
  3. Shortest-path traversal is run per target table under maxJoinHops .
  4. Resolved edges are merged without duplicates.
  5. Unresolved tables are captured and later enforced by AST validator.
 ---
7SemanticAstGenerationStage
**Responsibility:** Generate strict JSON AST using LLM. **Session Mutations:** astRawJson , astVersion , SemanticQueryAstV1 , canonicalAst . **Config/Table Dependencies:** ce_config prompt keys for DefaultSemanticAstGenerator , ce_audit . { AstGenerationResult generated = generator.generate( context.question(), context.retrieval(), context.joinPath(), context.session()); context.astGeneration(generated); if (generated != null && generated.ast() != null) { context.canonicalAst(astCanonicalizer.fromV1(generated.ast())); } } { String raw = llmClient.generateJsonStrict(session, llmPrompt, jsonSchema, ctxJson); SemanticQueryAstV1 ast = mapper.readValue(raw, SemanticQueryAstV1.class); ast = astNormalizer.normalize(ast, model, entity, session); publishLlmEvent(ConvEngineAuditStage.AST_OUTPUT.name(), session, false, llmOutputPayload); }
  1. Stage runs only after retrieval + join-path success.
  2. Prompt context includes question, selected entity, allowed entities, candidate tables, join path, and context json.
  3. Schema JSON is entity-specialized so generated fields stay within selected semantic entity.
  4. LLM output is strict JSON; SQL text output is not accepted in this stage.
  5. Raw JSON is normalized and audited via AST_INPUT , AST_OUTPUT , AST_ERROR determinants.
 ---
8SemanticAstValidationStage
**Responsibility:** Validate canonical AST against semantic contracts. **Session Mutations:** astValidation.valid , astValidation.errors . **Config/Table Dependencies:** semantic model fields/metrics/rules, ce_audit . { AstValidationResult result = validator.validate( context.canonicalAst(), model, context.joinPath(), context.session()); context.astValidation(result); if (!result.valid()) { throw new IllegalStateException("semantic AST validation failed: " + result.errors()); } } { validateExistsBlocks(errors, ast.existsBlocks(), model); validateSubqueryFilters(errors, ast.subqueryFilters(), fieldNames, model); validateWindows(errors, ast.windows(), fieldNames); if (ast.limit() > maxLimit) errors.add("limit exceeds max: " + ast.limit()); }
  1. Entity must exist in semantic model; unknown entity fails immediately.
  2. Projection/group/sort/filter field keys are validated against semantic fields.
  3. Operator-value shape is validated ( IN , BETWEEN , null-check operators, etc.).
  4. Exists/subquery/window clauses are recursively validated.
  5. Unresolved join-path tables and limit/offset constraints are enforced pre-compile.
 ---
9SemanticSqlCompileStage
**Responsibility:** Deterministic AST -> parameterized SQL compile + policy validation. **Session Mutations:** compiledSql , compiledSqlParams , sqlCompiled=true . **Config/Table Dependencies:** semantic model mappings, SQL clause/operator/window handlers, McpSqlGuardrail , ce_audit . { CompiledSql compiled = compiler.compile(context); resolveValidator(context).validate(compiled, context); context.compiledSql(compiled); publishCompiled(context.session(), compiled); } { CompileWorkPlan plan = new CompileWorkPlan(context, ast, model, entity, context.joinPath()); clauseHandlerRegistry.applyAll(plan); return plan.getCompiledSql(); } { sqlGuardrail.assertReadOnly(compiledSql.sql(), "semantic.query"); }
  1. Compile stage runs only after AST validation is successful.
  2. Compiler uses canonical AST and semantic model to resolve physical columns/tables.
  3. Clause handler registries apply predicates, metrics, windows, exists, and subqueries.
  4. Output SQL is always parameterized with named bind values.
  5. Policy validator applies read-only guardrail before execution stage.
 ---
10SemanticSqlExecuteStage
**Responsibility:** Execute compiled SQL and map result rows. **Session Mutations:** execution.rowCount , execution.rows[] , _db.sql , _db.params . **Config/Table Dependencies:** datasource/JDBC, domain tables (read-only), ce_audit . { SemanticSqlExecutor executor = resolveExecutor(context); SemanticExecutionResult result = executor.execute(context.compiledSql(), context); context.executionResult(result); publishExecuted(context, result); } { List> rows = jdbcTemplate.queryForList( compiledSql.sql(), compiledSql.params() == null ? Map.of() : compiledSql.params() ); return new SemanticExecutionResult(rows.size(), List.copyOf(rows)); }
  1. Stage fails fast if SQL compile output is missing.
  2. Named-parameter SQL executes through NamedParameterJdbcTemplate .
  3. Result rows are copied into immutable execution payload.
  4. Audit payload captures SQL and bind params under _db for debugging.
  5. DB grammar/type/permission errors are surfaced as SQL_EXECUTE_ERROR with root cause.
 ---
11SemanticResultSummaryStage
**Responsibility:** Build deterministic text/markdown summary from result rows. **Session Mutations:** summary . **Config/Table Dependencies:** execution result data, ce_audit . { SemanticResultSummarizer summarizer = resolveSummarizer(context); String summary = summarizer.summarize(context.executionResult(), context); context.summary(summary); } { if (result.rowCount() == 0) return "No matching records found."; return buildMarkdownTable(result); }
  1. No-result branch returns deterministic string, not LLM-generated prose.
  2. Result-table branch extracts headers from first row keys.
  3. Rows are serialized as markdown table to render in ConvEngine UI/docs trace.
  4. Cell escape logic keeps table valid for symbols/newlines.
  5. Summary preview + length are emitted into audit payload.
 ---
12McpPlanner + RulesStep (POST_AGENT_MCP)
**Responsibility:** Planner finalizes answer and rules complete post-MCP transitions. **Session Mutations:** mcp.finalAnswer , outcome/status flags, state transitions. **Config/Table Dependencies:** ce_mcp_planner , ce_rule , ce_audit . { if (McpConstants.ACTION_ANSWER.equalsIgnoreCase(plan.action())) { writeFinalAnswerToContext(session, plan.answer()); session.putInputParam(ConvEngineInputParamKey.MCP_FINAL_ANSWER, plan.answer()); break; } } { String phase = RulePhase.normalize(requestedPhase); List allRules = staticCacheService.findEligibleRules(passIntent, passState, phase); actionResolver.resolve(session, rule); }
  1. After semantic execution, planner receives fresh observations and decides final answer text.
  2. mcp.finalAnswer is stored in context and input params.
  3. POST_AGENT_MCP rule phase runs to move workflow state.
  4. Rule actions can map to success/failure/reset states.
  5. Planner and rule outcomes are auditable for RCA.
 ---
13ResponseResolutionStep + Assistant output
**Responsibility:** Resolve final response mapping and emit assistant payload. **Session Mutations:** OutputPayload + persisted turn output. **Config/Table Dependencies:** ce_response , ce_prompt_template , ce_output_schema (optional), ce_conversation , ce_audit . { Optional responseOptional = resolveResponse(session); CeResponse resp = responseOptional.get(); typeFactory.get(resp.getResponseType(), session) .resolve(session, PromptTemplate.initFrom(template), ResponseTemplate.initFrom(resp)); } { outputPayload.put(ConvEnginePayloadKey.OUTPUT, payloadValue); outputPayload.put(ConvEnginePayloadKey.OUTPUT_FORMAT, resp.getOutputFormat()); audit.audit(ConvEngineAuditStage.ASSISTANT_OUTPUT, session.getConversationId(), outputPayload); }
  1. Response row is selected by intent/state and priority.
  2. Response type handler renders EXACT/DERIVED payload accordingly.
  3. Output transformers are applied if configured.
  4. Final payload is attached to session and returned as assistant turn output.
  5. Assistant output determinant closes the turn-level audit trail.
## Semantic Model YAML ( semantic-layer.yaml ) Semantic mode relies on a declarative semantic model file that maps business language to physical schema. Retrieval, AST validation, join-path planning, metric compilation, and guardrails all consume this file. mcp.db.semantic.model-path=classpath:/mcp/semantic-layer.yaml ### Complete sample semantic model { version: 1 database: zapper_ops description: > Semantic model for Zapper disconnect workflow analytics and production diagnostics. This model maps user/business language (disconnect, downstream failure, BillBank gap, account status) to physical database tables and join paths. settings: default_limit: 100 timezone: UTC sql_dialect: postgres entities: Account: description: > Represents a Zapper customer billing account used as the anchor for request, order, and billing diagnostics. synonyms: - contract - subscription - service account - customer account - billing account example_questions: - show account details for UPSA100 - what is account status for ACMA200 - list accounts created this week - show billing cost for account UPSA100 tables: primary: zp_account related: - zp_customer fields: accountId: column: zp_account.account_id type: string description: unique account identifier used across request/order/billing tables searchable: true filterable: true example: UPSA100 customerId: column: zp_account.customer_id type: string description: owner customer identifier linked to zp_customer searchable: true filterable: true accountStatus: column: zp_account.account_status type: string description: account lifecycle status in Zapper filterable: true allowed_values: - ACTIVE - SUSPENDED - CLOSED currentBillingCost: column: zp_account.current_billing_cost type: number description: current computed billing cost amount for account createdAt: column: zp_account.created_at type: timestamp description: account creation timestamp DisconnectRequest: description: > Represents disconnect workflow requests and downstream order execution state. This is the main entity for "why disconnect failed" style analysis. synonyms: - disconnect - disconnection - service termination - cancel service - disconnect request - failed disconnect example_questions: - - zp_request - zp_disconnect_order - zp_order_downstream_check - zp_ui_data - zp_billbank_record deny_operations: - DELETE - UPDATE - DROP max_result_limit: 500 } ## semantic-layer.yaml This section explains every major tag in the full sample and what ConvEngine actually does with it at runtime. SemanticModelLoader is configured with FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES=false . That means unknown/unmapped YAML tags are ignored safely at load time. ### 1) Model identity tags { version: 1 database: zapper_ops description: Semantic model for Zapper disconnect workflow analytics. } What this does: - version : surfaces as semanticModelVersion in runtime output and stage payloads. - database + description : metadata/context only; not a SQL generator by itself. ### 2) entities block { entities: DisconnectRequest: description: Main disconnect workflow entity synonyms: [disconnect, disconnection, service termination] tables: primary: zp_request related: [zp_disconnect_order, zp_order_downstream_check, zp_ui_data] fields: requestId: column: zp_request.zp_request_id type: string key: true searchable: true filterable: true teamNotes: column: zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes type: string failureReason: column: zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes type: string } How each child tag is used: ", "retrieval + validator", "Becomes candidate entity and AST entity target."], ["description", "retrieval + LLM prompt", "Improves retrieval relevance and LLM grounding context."], ["synonyms", "retrieval (+ embedding text)", "Raises deterministic synonym score for candidate ranking."], ["tables.primary", "planner/compiler/validator", "Primary base table candidate for joins and projections."], ["tables.related", "retrieval/planner", "Expands table candidates for join-path resolution."], ["fields..column", "compiler", "Maps semantic field key to physical table.column in SQL select/where/order/group."], ["fields..type", "validator", "Operator/type validation (e.g., ILIKE requires string-like field)."], ["fields..description", "retrieval", "Extra lexical signal for entity/field matching."], ["fields..aliases", "normalizer + LLM prompt context", "Maps user/LLM variants (for example id/status/created) to canonical semantic field keys."], ["fields..filterable/searchable/key", "currently metadata", "Carried in model; not strict query gating in current validator/compiler."] ]} /> Example: - User asks: show requests where teamNotes contains billing - Retrieval picks DisconnectRequest (field ownership + lexical/synonym/vector signals). - AST schema specialization restricts allowed field keys for selected entity. - Compiler uses teamNotes -> zp_ui_data.zp_asr_team_notes and, if filters reference another table-owned field (for example requestId -> zp_request.zp_request_id ), compile stage auto-expands required tables and adds the join path. ### 3) tables + columns + foreign_key { tables: zp_request: description: disconnect request master table columns: zp_request_id: type: varchar primary_key: true account_id: type: varchar foreign_key: table: zp_account column: account_id request_status: type: varchar requested_at: type: timestamptz } What this does: - Provides table/column metadata for schema validation and graph wiring. - primary_key and foreign_key are model-level structure hints. - foreign_key.table/column can contribute to relationship sanity and graph traversal completeness. ### 4) relationships { relationships: - name: request_to_disconnect_order description: request to downstream disconnect order from: { table: zp_request, column: zp_request_id } to: { table: zp_disconnect_order, column: zp_request_id } type: one_to_many } What this does: - Join-path stage builds traversal edges from these mappings. - SQL compiler uses edges to generate deterministic joins. - Correlation predicates for EXISTS/subqueries also rely on relationship knowledge. ### 5) metrics { metrics: billbank_gap_count: description: count of disconnect orders missing billbank linkage sql: | COUNT(CASE WHEN zp_disconnect_order.zp_disconnect_id IS NOT NULL AND zp_billbank_record.zp_disconnect_id IS NULL THEN 1 END) } What this does: - Metric key becomes allowed value in AST metrics[] . - Metric SQL is inserted by compiler when aggregation is requested. - Having clause can reference metric keys. Critical rule: - If metrics is present with detail projections, selected fields must be grouped ( group_by ) or query is invalid. ### 6) synonyms (top-level) { synonyms: disconnect: [service termination, disconnection] billing: [billbank, invoice, reconciliation] } Current behavior: - Loaded into model and available for future expansion. - Main retrieval scoring today relies primarily on entity-level synonyms + fields + descriptions. ### 7) value_patterns + intent_rules (deterministic bridge for ambiguous asks) { value_patterns: - from_field: requestId to_field: disconnectOrderId value_starts_with: [DON] - from_field: disconnectOrderId to_field: requestId value_starts_with: [ZPR] intent_rules: don_failure_reason_details: description: stabilize DON-level failure asks to disconnect-order diagnostics output match_any: [why did, fail, failed, failure reason] must_contain: [don] force_entity: DisconnectRequest force_mode: detail force_select: [disconnectOrderId, downstreamStatus, failureReason, requestId] request_failure_reason_details: description: stabilize request-level failure-reason asks to request diagnostics output match_any: [failure reason, reason for request, why request failed, failed reason] must_contain: [request] force_entity: DisconnectRequest force_mode: detail force_select: [requestId, failureReason] billbank_gap_details: description: force billbank gap details flow match_any: [billbank gap, billbank gaps, billing gap, billing gaps] must_contain: [request, order, detail] force_entity: DisconnectRequest force_mode: detail force_mode_even_with_metrics: true force_select: [requestId, accountId, requestStatus, requestedAt, disconnectOrderId, downstreamStatus, disconnectId, teamNotes] enforce_where: - field: disconnectId op: IS_NULL value: null } What this does at runtime: - match_any + must_contain gates when rule activates. - force_entity overrides LLM-selected entity. - force_mode: detail drops accidental aggregate pieces ( metrics , having , group_by ). - force_mode_even_with_metrics: true allows this rule to force detail/select even if AST contains metrics . - force_select sets deterministic projection for that business intent. - enforce_where injects deterministic filters. - Global value_patterns applies deterministic filter-field remap based on value prefix (example requestId + DON* -> disconnectOrderId ) without hardcoding in framework. - Rule-level field_remaps is still supported for rule-specific overrides when needed. SemanticField supports optional aliases in YAML. SemanticModel supports top-level value_patterns (global remaps), and SemanticIntentRule supports optional field_remaps (rule-specific remaps), all mapped via SemanticIntentFieldRemap . ### 8) settings , join_hints , rules , and other descriptive tags { settings: default_limit: 100 timezone: UTC sql_dialect: postgres join_hints: zp_request: commonly_joined_with: [zp_disconnect_order, zp_ui_data] rules: allowed_tables: [zp_request, zp_disconnect_order] deny_operations: [DELETE, UPDATE, DROP] max_result_limit: 500 } Current status: - These tags are excellent documentation for model authors. - They are not currently bound to SemanticModel records, so runtime ignores them. - Effective runtime limit/timezone/dialect come from application.yml ( convengine.mcp.db.semantic.* ). - DML/DDL guardrails are enforced by SQL policy/guardrail steps, not YAML rules . Other descriptive tags in sample: - example_questions , allowed_values , example are currently prompt/documentation aids and are not strict runtime-enforced model fields. ## AST + SQL impact walkthrough User question: - show billbank gaps with request and order details LLM generated AST (problem shape): { { "entity": "BillingRecord", "select": ["billbankId","accountId","disconnectId","recordStatus","pendingBillAmount","updatedAt"], "group_by": [], "metrics": ["billbank_gap_count"], "having": { "op": "AND", "conditions": [{"field":"billbank_gap_count","op":">","value":0}], "groups": [] } } } Why validator rejects: - Metrics + detailed select fields requires grouping. - With empty group_by , SQL semantics are invalid. With current normalizer fix: - Mixed aggregate/detail AST is demoted to detail query. - metrics/having/group_by are removed deterministically before validation. { { "entity": "BillingRecord", "select": ["billbankId","accountId","disconnectId","recordStatus","pendingBillAmount","updatedAt"], "group_by": [], "metrics": [], "having": {"op":"AND","conditions":[],"groups":[]} } } Typical SQL shape after fix: { select t0.billbank_id as billbankId, t0.account_id as accountId, t0.zp_disconnect_id as disconnectId, t0.record_status as recordStatus, t0.pending_bill_amount as pendingBillAmount, t0.updated_at as updatedAt from zp_billbank_record t0 limit :__limit } If you want aggregate output intentionally, prompt explicitly: - show billbank gap count by accountId ## Semantic Query Tool Runtime Deep Dive This section explains the runtime tool path that starts at db.semantic.query and executes retrieval -> join path -> AST -> SQL -> summary in deterministic stage order. Runtime files below show how the semantic tool call is orchestrated from handler entry to summarized result. ### High-level code execution order (runtime)
1DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java receives tool request and calls runtime service.
2Runtime orchestration handoff flows into Result Summary/ Wrapper Deep Dive where wrapper behavior is documented.
3Interceptors: SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java + DefaultSemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java.
4Pipeline wiring: SemanticStagePipelineFactory.java -> SemanticStagePipeline.java.
5Stage contract/context: SemanticQueryStage.java + SemanticQueryContext.java.
6Execution stages: SemanticRetrievalStage.java -> SemanticJoinPathStage.java -> SemanticAstGenerationStage.java -> SemanticAstValidationStage.java -> SemanticSqlCompileStage.java -> SemanticSqlExecuteStage.java -> SemanticResultSummaryStage.java.
#### DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java
{ // DbSemanticQueryToolHandler.java // Purpose: MCP tool handler entrypoint for semantic query execution. // Flow 1: Accepts tool args and extracts the semantic question payload. // Flow 2: Delegates planning/execution to SemanticQueryRuntimeService. // Flow 3: Returns normalized tool result envelope to MCP step. package com.github.salilvnair.convengine.engine.mcp.query.semantic.runtime.handler; @Component @RequiredArgsConstructor @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix = "convengine.mcp.db.semantic", name = "enabled", havingValue = "true") // Primary type declaration for this component. public class DbSemanticQueryToolHandler implements DbToolHandler { private final ConvEngineMcpConfig mcpConfig; private final SemanticQueryRuntimeService runtimeService; @Override // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. public String toolCode() { ConvEngineMcpConfig.Db.Semantic cfg = mcpConfig.getDb() == null ? new ConvEngineMcpConfig.Db.Semantic() : mcpConfig.getDb().getSemantic(); return cfg.getToolCode() == null || cfg.getToolCode().isBlank() ? "db.semantic.query" : cfg.getToolCode(); } @Override // Executes core stage/operation logic and mutates shared context/state. public Object execute(CeMcpTool tool, Map args, EngineSession session) { String question = extractQuestion(args, session); return runtimeService.plan(question, session); } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private String extractQuestion(Map args, EngineSession session) { if (args != null) { Object query = args.get("question"); if (query == null) { query = args.get("query"); } if (query == null) { query = args.get("user_input"); } if (query != null && !String.valueOf(query).isBlank()) { return String.valueOf(query); } } return session == null ? "" : session.getUserText(); } } } **What this file does:** - Runtime role: MCP tool handler entrypoint for semantic query execution. - Detailed control flow: - 1) Receives db.semantic.query tool args, delegates to runtime service, and returns structured semantic result envelope. - 2) Integrates with stage audit trail and deterministic runtime transitions. #### SemanticQueryStageInterceptor.java

Retrieval runs before join-path and determines which entity/table context is handed to AST generation. ### High-level code execution order
1SemanticEntityTableRetriever.java entry contract is invoked by SemanticRetrievalStage.
2Hooks run via SemanticRetrievalInterceptor.java (before/after/error).
3DefaultSemanticEntityTableRetriever.java computes deterministic lexical/field/id scores.
4Optional vector path via SemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java and PostgresPgVectorSearchAdapter.java.
5Fallback/no-vector behavior via NoopSemanticVectorSearchAdapter.java.
6Rank rows materialize into CandidateEntity.java and CandidateTable.java.
7Final envelope emitted as RetrievalResult.java to join-path stage.

"" : entityName.toLowerCase(Locale.ROOT); double score = 0.0d; if (requestId && lower.contains("request")) { score += 1.0d; } if (donId && (lower.contains("disconnect") || lower.contains("order"))) { score += 1.0d; } if (disconnectId && (lower.contains("disconnect") || lower.contains("billing"))) { score += 1.0d; } return Math.min(1.0d, score / 2.0d); } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private double overlapScore(Set queryTokens, Set docTokens) { if (queryTokens == null || queryTokens.isEmpty() || docTokens == null || docTokens.isEmpty()) { return 0.0d; Join-path logic decides table traversal; SQL compiler then renders joins from this resolved plan. ### High-level code execution order (join-path)
1Graph contracts: SchemaGraphEngine.java, SchemaEdge.java, SchemaGraphInterceptor.java.
2Resolver contracts: JoinPathResolver.java -> JoinPathPlan.java.
3Planner contracts: AstPlanner.java + AstPlanningInterceptor.java.
4Provider implementations: JGraphTSchemaGraphEngine.java + DefaultJoinPathResolver.java.
5Planning providers: DefaultAstPlanner.java + DefaultAstPlanningInterceptor.java + DefaultSchemaGraphInterceptor.java.

Map nextEdges = new LinkedHashMap<>(); for (Map row : joins) { String sourceTable = text(row.get("source_table")); String sourceColumn = text(row.get("source_column")); String targetTable = text(row.get("target_table")); String targetColumn = text(row.get("target_column")); if (sourceTable.isBlank() || targetTable.isBlank()) { continue; } nextGraph.addVertex(sourceTable); nextGraph.addVertex(targetTable); DefaultEdge edge = nextGraph.addEdge(sourceTable, targetTable); } if (fromTable.equalsIgnoreCase(toTable)) { return List.of(); } if (!graph.containsVertex(fromTable) || !graph.containsVertex(toTable)) { return List.of(); } record NodePath(String node, List path) {} java.util.ArrayDeque queue = new java.util.ArrayDeque<>(); Set visited = new LinkedHashSet<>(); queue.add(new NodePath(fromTable, List.of(fromTable))); visited.add(fromTable); while (!queue.isEmpty()) { NodePath current = queue.poll(); if (current.node().equals(toTable)) { return toEdges(current.path()); } var metric = model.metrics().get(metricName); if (metric == null || metric.sql() == null || metric.sql().isBlank()) { return; } Matcher matcher = TABLE_TOKEN_PATTERN.matcher(metric.sql()); while (matcher.find()) { String table = matcher.group(1); if (table != null && !table.isBlank()) { tables.add(table); } } } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private void addFieldTable(Set tables, SemanticField field) { if (field == null || field.column() == null || field.column().isBlank()) { return; } String[] parts = field.column().split("\\."); if (parts.length == 2 && !parts[0].isBlank()) { tables.add(parts[0]); } } private List buildEffectiveEdges(String baseTable, Set requiredTables, List joinEdges, SemanticModel model) { List existing = joinEdges == null ? new ArrayList<>() : new ArrayList<>(joinEdges); if (requiredTables == null || requiredTables.isEmpty()) { return existing; } Set connected = connectedTables(baseTable, existing); connected.add(baseTable); List out = new ArrayList<>(existing); for (String target : requiredTables) { if (target == null || target.isBlank() || connected.contains(target)) { continue; } List path = shortestPath(model, connected, target); if (path.isEmpty()) { continue; } for (SchemaEdge edge : path) { if (!containsEdge(out, edge)) { out.add(edge); } connected.add(edge.leftTable()); connected.add(edge.rightTable()); } } return out; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private Set connectedTables(String base, List edges) { Set connected = new LinkedHashSet<>(); if (base != null) connected.add(base); boolean changed; do { changed = false; for (SchemaEdge edge : edges) { if (edge == null) continue; boolean left = connected.contains(edge.leftTable()); boolean right = connected.contains(edge.rightTable()); if (left && !right) changed = connected.add(edge.rightTable()) || changed; else if (right && !left) changed = connected.add(edge.leftTable()) || changed; } } while (changed); return connected; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private boolean containsEdge(List edges, SchemaEdge candidate) { for (SchemaEdge e : edges) { if (e == null || candidate == null) continue; if (Objects.equals(e.leftTable(), candidate.leftTable()) && Objects.equals(e.leftColumn(), candidate.leftColumn()) && Objects.equals(e.rightTable(), candidate.rightTable()) && Objects.equals(e.rightColumn(), candidate.rightColumn())) { return true; } } return false; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private List shortestPath(SemanticModel model, Set starts, String target) { List graphEdges = semanticGraphEdges(model); if (graphEdges.isEmpty()) { return List.of(); } Set startSet = starts == null || starts.isEmpty() ? Set.of(target) : starts; Queue queue = new ArrayDeque<>(startSet); Map prev = new LinkedHashMap<>(); Map prevEdge = new LinkedHashMap<>(); Set visited = new HashSet<>(startSet); while (!queue.isEmpty()) { String cur = queue.poll(); if (Objects.equals(cur, target)) { break; } for (SchemaEdge edge : graphEdges) { if (edge == null) continue; String a = edge.leftTable(); String b = edge.rightTable(); if (a == null || b == null) { continue; } if (a.equals(cur) && !visited.contains(b)) { visited.add(b); queue.add(b); prev.put(b, a); prevEdge.put(b, edge); } if (b.equals(cur) && !visited.contains(a)) { visited.add(a); queue.add(a); prev.put(a, b); prevEdge.put(a, new SchemaEdge( b, edge.rightColumn(), a, edge.leftColumn(), edge.source(), edge.joinType() )); } } } if (!visited.contains(target) || startSet.contains(target)) { return List.of(); } List path = new ArrayList<>(); String node = target; while (!startSet.contains(node)) { SchemaEdge edge = prevEdge.get(node); if (edge == null) { return List.of(); } path.add(0, edge); node = prev.get(node); if (node == null) { return List.of(); } } return path; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private List semanticGraphEdges(SemanticModel model) { if (model == null) { return List.of(); } List edges = new ArrayList<>(); if (model.relationships() != null) { for (SemanticRelationship rel : model.relationships()) { if (rel == null || rel.from() == null || rel.to() == null) { continue; } String a = rel.from().table(); String b = rel.to().table(); String aCol = rel.from().column(); String bCol = rel.to().column(); if (a == null || b == null || aCol == null || bCol == null) { continue; } addGraphEdge(edges, new SchemaEdge(a, aCol, b, bCol, "relationship", "LEFT")); } } if (model.tables() != null) { for (Map.Entry tableEntry : model.tables().entrySet()) { String fkTable = tableEntry.getKey(); var table = tableEntry.getValue(); if (fkTable == null || table == null || table.columns() == null) { continue; } for (Map.Entry colEntry : table.columns().entrySet()) { String fkColumn = colEntry.getKey(); var column = colEntry.getValue(); if (fkColumn == null || column == null || column.foreignKey() == null) { continue; } String pkTable = column.foreignKey().table(); String pkColumn = column.foreignKey().column(); if (pkTable == null || pkColumn == null) { continue; } addGraphEdge(edges, new SchemaEdge(pkTable, pkColumn, fkTable, fkColumn, "foreign_key", "LEFT")); } } } return edges; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private void addGraphEdge(List edges, SchemaEdge candidate) { if (edges == null || candidate == null) { return; } for (SchemaEdge edge : edges) { if (edge == null) continue; if (Objects.equals(edge.leftTable(), candidate.leftTable()) && Objects.equals(edge.leftColumn(), candidate.leftColumn()) && Objects.equals(edge.rightTable(), candidate.rightTable()) && Objects.equals(edge.rightColumn(), candidate.rightColumn())) { return; } } edges.add(candidate); } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private Map buildAliases(String baseTable, List edges) { Map alias = new LinkedHashMap<>(); alias.put(baseTable, "t0"); int idx = 1; for (SchemaEdge e : edges) { if (!alias.containsKey(e.leftTable())) { alias.put(e.leftTable(), "t" + idx++); } if (!alias.containsKey(e.rightTable())) { alias.put(e.rightTable(), "t" + idx++); } } return alias; } // Helper method used by the main runtime flow in this component. private Table tableRef(String tableName, String alias) { if (alias == null || alias.isBlank()) { return DSL.table(DSL.name(tableName)); } return DSL.table(DSL.name(tableName)).as(alias); } private org.jooq.Field

title: mcp audit
content: MCP, Tool Orchestration, and Audit (v2) Two tool paths 1. ToolOrchestrationStep (direct tool mode) request driven via tool_request executes one tool runs POST_TOOL_EXECUTION rules writes context.mcp.toolExecution. 2. McpToolStep (planner loop mode) planner loop ( CALL_TOOL / ANSWER ) writes context.mcp.observations[] and context.mcp.finalAnswer runs POST_AGENT_MCP rules writes context.mcp.lifecycle. Guardrail blocked branch If planner proposes a blocked next tool: mcp_status=GUARDRAIL_BLOCKED_NEXT_TOOL fallback final text is written to context.mcp.finalAnswer POST_AGENT_MCP rules still run response is still finalized by ce_response resolution Rule ready MCP metadata context.mcp.lifecycle includes: phase , status , outcome , finished blocked , error , errorMessage lastAction , lastToolCode , lastToolGroup , lastToolArgs toolExecuted context.mcp.toolExecution includes: phase , status , outcome , finished error , scopeMismatch , toolExecuted toolCode , toolGroup , meta , result , errorMessage JSON_PATH examples for ce_rule.match_pattern text $[?(@.context.mcp.lifecycle.finished == true && @.context.mcp.lifecycle.outcome == 'BLOCKED')] $[?(@.context.mcp.lifecycle.error == true)] $[?(@.context.mcp.toolExecution.phase == 'POST_TOOL_EXECUTION' && @.context.mcp.toolExecution.status == 'SUCCESS')] $[?(@.context.mcp.toolExecution.scopeMismatch == true)] Audit stages to watch Tool orchestration path: TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_REQUEST TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_RESULT TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_ERROR Planner MCP path: MCP_PLAN_LLM_INPUT MCP_PLAN_LLM_OUTPUT MCP_TOOL_CALL MCP_TOOL_RESULT MCP_TOOL_ERROR MCP_FINAL_ANSWER Rule phases around these paths: RULE_MATCH (McpToolStep) / RULE_NO_MATCH (McpToolStep) RULE_MATCH (ToolOrchestrationStep PostTool) / RULE_NO_MATCH (ToolOrchestrationStep PostTool) RULE_MATCH (AgentIntentResolver) / RULE_NO_MATCH (AgentIntentResolver) RULE_MATCH (RulesStep) / RULE_NO_MATCH (RulesStep) Scope enforcement ce_mcp_tool and ce_mcp_planner are scope validated at startup: null/blank scope rows are rejected intent_code must be ANY , UNKNOWN , or defined in ce_intent state_code must be ANY , UNKNOWN , or present in ce_rule.state_code
rawContent: # MCP, Tool Orchestration, and Audit (v2) ## Two tool paths 1. ToolOrchestrationStep (direct tool mode) - request-driven via tool_request - executes one tool - runs POST_TOOL_EXECUTION rules - writes context.mcp.toolExecution.* 2. McpToolStep (planner loop mode) - planner loop ( CALL_TOOL / ANSWER ) - writes context.mcp.observations[] and context.mcp.finalAnswer - runs POST_AGENT_MCP rules - writes context.mcp.lifecycle.* ## Guardrail blocked branch If planner proposes a blocked next tool: - mcp_status=GUARDRAIL_BLOCKED_NEXT_TOOL - fallback final text is written to context.mcp.finalAnswer - POST_AGENT_MCP rules still run - response is still finalized by ce_response resolution ## Rule-ready MCP metadata context.mcp.lifecycle includes: - phase , status , outcome , finished - blocked , error , errorMessage - lastAction , lastToolCode , lastToolGroup , lastToolArgs - toolExecuted context.mcp.toolExecution includes: - phase , status , outcome , finished - error , scopeMismatch , toolExecuted - toolCode , toolGroup , meta , result , errorMessage ## JSON_PATH examples for ce_rule.match_pattern text $[?(@.context.mcp.lifecycle.finished == true && @.context.mcp.lifecycle.outcome == 'BLOCKED')] $[?(@.context.mcp.lifecycle.error == true)] $[?(@.context.mcp.toolExecution.phase == 'POST_TOOL_EXECUTION' && @.context.mcp.toolExecution.status == 'SUCCESS')] $[?(@.context.mcp.toolExecution.scopeMismatch == true)] ## Audit stages to watch Tool orchestration path: - TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_REQUEST - TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_RESULT - TOOL_ORCHESTRATION_ERROR Planner MCP path: - MCP_PLAN_LLM_INPUT - MCP_PLAN_LLM_OUTPUT - MCP_TOOL_CALL - MCP_TOOL_RESULT - MCP_TOOL_ERROR - MCP_FINAL_ANSWER Rule phases around these paths: - RULE_MATCH (McpToolStep) / RULE_NO_MATCH (McpToolStep) - RULE_MATCH (ToolOrchestrationStep PostTool) / RULE_NO_MATCH (ToolOrchestrationStep PostTool) - RULE_MATCH (AgentIntentResolver) / RULE_NO_MATCH (AgentIntentResolver) - RULE_MATCH (RulesStep) / RULE_NO_MATCH (RulesStep) ## Scope enforcement ce_mcp_tool and ce_mcp_planner are scope-validated at startup: - null/blank scope rows are rejected - intent_code must be ANY , UNKNOWN , or defined in ce_intent - state_code must be ANY , UNKNOWN , or present in ce_rule.state_code
